text.title: Continuous Discovery Antipatterns
text.summary: Common failure modes in continuous discovery practices and how to detect and prevent them.

antipattern.description: Discovery theatre: weekly interviews held but never tied back to decisions. The cadence tracks attendance, not impact.
antipattern.reason: Discovery that generates insights nobody acts on wastes researcher time and erodes team confidence in the practice. Require each interview cycle to produce at least one tagged opportunity or a documented null result.

antipattern.description: Same 5 power users interviewed every week, producing a confirmation chamber.
antipattern.reason: Power users are not representative users. Their advanced usage patterns actively mislead feature prioritization. Rotate the participant panel on a fixed schedule.

antipattern.description: Discovery and delivery teams operating as separate units: discovery becomes a wishlist generator nobody implements.
antipattern.reason: Discovery findings must map directly to the team that makes build decisions. Separate org structures create a handoff gap where insights are filtered by priority politics.

antipattern.description: Synthesis agent over-summarises: many distinct interview quotes collapse into 2-3 generic themes.
antipattern.reason: Generic themes lose actionable signal. Set a minimum number of distinct themes and require evidence count per theme before accepting synthesis output.

antipattern.description: Sentiment classifier reports high confidence on small samples (n less than 15).
antipattern.reason: Statistical significance requires sufficient sample size. Refuse confidence above 0.8 below n=15 and flag sample size in all synthesis output.

antipattern.description: Agents recommend "schedule more interviews" as the remedy for any gap in understanding.
antipattern.reason: Interviews are expensive. Behavioural data and support ticket analysis should be the first resort; interviews are the escalation path when data is ambiguous.

antipattern.description: Agent pushes interview transcripts with interviewee names to shared repositories without redaction.
antipattern.reason: PII leakage via research tools is a trust and legal risk. Add a scrubber step before any transcript touches an external repo.
